blocks.content: Agents maintain conversation context using memory strategies. The Memory tab of `adk-web` provides a real-time view of active variables, context buffers, and sliding window indices.
The memory visualizer maps active messages loaded in current model requests vs compressed history blocks. You can view:

items: Compacted summary notes injected as system context.
Active session keys (such as user authentication metadata).

blocks.title: Compaction Thresholds
blocks.content: The compaction indicator flashes yellow when the current conversation history approaches 80% of the configured sliding window limit, warning that a context compression run is imminent.
Question 1: What indicator tells you that the agent's active memory is approaching compaction?

- A) The server restarts automatically.
- B) The compaction indicator changes color (yellow warning) as history size nears 80% of the window limits.
- C) The adk CLI commands return error codes.
Feedback: Option B is correct. adk-web highlights context size usage warnings visual indicator when nearing compression boundaries.
